perturbation.original_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, only during their employment with AUGI following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the confidentiality restriction to 'only during their employment with AUGI' creates a contradiction with the broader intent of protecting AUGI's confidential information even after the stockholders leave the company. This change weakens the non-disclosure agreement and contradicts the materiality of the agreement as described in Section 3(a) where the protection of this information was a condition of the merger. This means information can be exposed at any time as soon the employee leaves.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Material Agreement. (a) It is understood by and between the parties hereto that the foregoing -2- <PAGE> covenants by the Stockholders are essential elements of this Agreement and that but for the agreements of the Stockholders to comply with such covenants, AUGI would not have entered into the Merger Agreement or paid the Merger Consideration to the Stockholders. AUGI and the Stockholders have independently consulted with their respective counsel and have been advised concerning the reasonableness and propriety of such covenants with specific regard to the businesses conducted by the Company. (b) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), no future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the clause 'only during their employment with AUGI following the effectiveness of the Merger'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that the Stockholders' covenants are 'essential elements of this Agreement and that but for the agreements of the Stockholders to comply with such covenants, AUGI would not have entered into the Merger Agreement'. The contradicted text emphasizes the ongoing nature of the agreement, whereas the changed text limits the confidentiality restriction to the employment period. This creates a significant inconsistency, as the original agreement was predicated on the continuous protection of AUGI's confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information which information and its relevance in the particular instance (A) is a matter of common knowledge or public record, (B) is or becomes generally available to the public as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder, (C) which information and its relevance is generally known in Business, (D) the Stockholder can demonstrate that such information and its relevance in the applicable instance, was already known to the recipient thereof other than by reason of any breach of any obligation of the Stockholder under this Agreement or any other confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement, or (E) the information is required to be disclosed by law or pursuant to court order or subpoeona.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder' from the exception to 'Confidential Information' directly contradicts the intent of protecting AUGI's confidential information. Now, if a stockholder discloses information, and it becomes public as a result of that disclosure, it's no longer considered confidential under the agreement. This introduces a loophole that allows stockholders to circumvent the non-disclosure agreement by disclosing information themselves and then claiming it's publicly available. This undermines the entire premise of Section 2 and the reliance on it described in Section 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Stockholders severally agrees that such Stockholder shall not, at any time following the effectiveness of the Merger, print, publish, divulge or communicate to any person, firm, corporation or other business organization or use for his or their own account any business contacts, customers, suppliers, technology, product designs and specifications, know-how, trade secrets, marketing techniques, promotional materials or other secret or confidential information relating to the Business or any other businesses then being engaged in by AUGI or any of its subsidiaries, or any secret or confidential information relating to the affairs, dealings and concerns of the Company, AUGI or any of the subsidiaries of AUGI (collectively, the "Confidential Information") which the Stockholder has received or obtained while an employee or member of the Board of Directors of AUGI or the Company (whether or not developed, devised, or otherwise created in whole or in part by the efforts of the Stockholder).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'other than as a result of disclosure by the Stockholder' from the definition of 'Confidential Information.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which prohibits stockholders from divulging 'Confidential Information.' The change allows a stockholder to disclose confidential information, making it publicly available, and then argue that it's no longer confidential, thus circumventing the non-disclosure agreement. This undermines the purpose of the contradicted section, which aims to protect AUGI's confidential information, as it creates a loophole where disclosure by the Stockholder removes the confidential status, conflicting directly with the restriction on disclosure outlined in the contradicted text. This is a clear and direct conflict affecting enforceability, aligning with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), no future act of omission or commission by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), a material future act of omission or commission by AUGI related directly to Section 1 and 2 of the agreement by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the word 'material' and including a direct relationship between the breaches to sections 1 and 2 introduces ambiguity into Section 3(b). Originally, it stated that *no* act or breach by AUGI could modify the Stockholders' non-compete/non-disclosure obligations. The modification now suggests that a *material* breach related directly to sections 1 and 2 by AUGI *could* potentially modify or terminate those obligations, creating uncertainty and contradiction of the absolute nature in the original text. This undermines the enforceability of sections 1 and 2 and provides a possible defense for the Stockholders to claim those sections no longer apply if AUGI commits a material breach directly related to non-compete/non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, express or implied, contained in this Agreement, the Merger Agreement or in any other "Transaction Document" (as that term is defined in the Merger Agreement), a material future act of omission or commission by AUGI related directly to Section 1 and 2 of the agreement by AUGI or any of its affiliates, or other actual or alleged breach by AUGI or such affiliates of any of their covenants or agreements contained in any Transaction Documents (in addition to this Agreement), shall in any way be deemed to modify, limit or terminate the covenants and agreements of the Stockholders contained in Section 1 and Section 2 hereof, all of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that *no* act or breach by AUGI could modify the Stockholders' non-compete/non-disclosure obligations. The changed text now suggests that a *material* breach related directly to sections 1 and 2 by AUGI *could* potentially modify or terminate those obligations. This change introduces ambiguity and contradiction as it creates uncertainty where there was none before. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the breach or threatened breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case without the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) intentionally breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the intentional breach or threatened intentional breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case with the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the word 'intentionally' and 'with the necessity of proving damages or posting bond' into Section 4 creates an internal contradiction by altering the conditions under which injunctive relief can be sought. Originally, AUGI could seek injunctive relief for any breach, without proving damages or posting a bond. Now, they can only do so for *intentional* breaches, and *must* prove damages or post a bond. This makes it more difficult for AUGI to enforce the agreement, weakening the protections afforded to them and contradicting the original intention of the contract to safeguard AUGI's interests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without intending to limit the remedies available to AUGI or the Company, each Stockholder further agrees that damages at law will be an insufficient remedy to AUGI if such Stockholder(s) intentionally breaches the terms of this Agreement and that AUGI or the Company may apply for and have injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction to restrain the intentional breach or threatened intentional breach of or otherwise to specifically enforce any of the covenants contained herein, in each case with the necessity of proving damages or posting bond.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which AUGI can seek injunctive relief. Originally, AUGI could seek injunctive relief for any breach. The changed text now requires the breach to be 'intentional' and necessitates proving damages or posting a bond, which directly contradicts the original terms that did not have these requirements. The original agreement provided AUGI with stronger protections, while the altered version weakens these protections. The contradictory text reinforces this by repeating the altered conditions. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the conditions for injunctive relief are defined inconsistently.
